Das, Litton BAN vs pace

Bangladesh M · RHB · How to exploit — pace plan

of team, per innings

innings with ≥25% of team
3-yr 18%

Summary

  • A solid, aggressive batter — strong vs pace, tight technique.
  • Plan for pace: bowl good length pitching outside off while seaming in.
  • They get out most often to balls that are back of a length and outside off (3.8 dismissals/100).
  • Most often out caught behind (31% of dismissals); most often to right fast (41).

Common themes

  • right-hand bat, averaging 30.6 at a strike rate of 64.8.
  • Carries a big share of the batting — 14.7% of team runs.
  • Scores mainly through the off side (43%).

Strengths

  • Main scoring shot is the work/nudge (34% of runs).
  • The pull/hook brings them 25% of their runs vs pace — 1.7× the typical Test batter's share.
  • Starts securely — their dismissal rate barely drops once set (1.9 early vs 2.3/100); early pressure alone won't buy their wicket. Early runs come mainly off the drive (43%).

Weaknesses / how to get him

  • Most often out caught behind (31% of dismissals); most often to right fast (41).
  • Vulnerable to the good length (averages 24 there vs 31 overall; 1.9 dismissals/100).
  • Struggles outside off (20 avg, 2.4 outs/100).
  • The ball that seams in hurts them (avg 13 vs 47 when it holds its line).
  • Most dangerous ball: back of a length and outside off (3.8 dismissals/100, avg 33).
  • His cut is high-risk (false shot 25%, 4.2 outs/100).

Match Impact (share of runs)

Makes 14.7% of their team's runs over a career (typical innings 11.9%), and 3.8% of all runs in their matches; carries the innings (≥25% of the team's runs) 18% of the time, and dominates (≥40%) 6%.

Suggested Fields (the stock field ± their evidenced deviations)

Each field starts from the stock template for that bowler type & phase (orthodox because it works), then makes at most three changes — each one earned by a trigger in their game clearing the cohort P75 bar (do they lap, cut, pull, score square, edge early?). Change rows are highlighted with their stat; Stock rows carry the orthodoxy line. The read line backtests the changes against the pure stock field. Early = first 30 balls; Set = once in. Drawn from behind the bowler (bowler bottom, striker top); off side is on the left.
Field vs right-arm pace
Early — first 30 balls · 1066 balls
Stock field + 1 change (highlighted) — covering +11% of their boundary runs vs the pure stock field.

Floating fielder: They score only 5% of their runs where mid-on stands — the spare run-saver to move as the game asks.
Once set · 1027 balls
Leg-side stock field — strong through the leg side (37% of their runs vs 24% off) — a squarer leg-side ring, one slip out. + 2 changes (highlighted) — covering +30% of their boundary runs vs the pure stock field.

Bouncer plan
A genuine plan — heavy puller/hooker (P90 vs pace). One slip, a catcher in front of square, and the two legal leg-side riders (deep backward square + deep fine leg).
FielderStock/ChangeWhy they're there
Slip 1ChangeOne slip kept for the top / outside edge.
Short legChangeCatcher in front of square for the fend or glove off the hip.
GullyChangeThe steer or fend that flies squarer of the wicket.
PointChangeRing saver for the bumper plan.
CoverChangeRing saver for the bumper plan.
Mid-offChangeRing saver for the bumper plan.
Mid-onChangeRing saver for the bumper plan.
Deep backward squareChangeDeep behind square for the top-edged pull.
Deep fine legChangeDeep behind square for the hook.
